reference: GATE CS — Computer Networks / Data Link Layer

formulas.name: Cyclic Redundancy Check (CRC)
formulas.plain: Transmit = M(x)*x^r minus (M(x)*x^r mod G(x)); r = degree of generator G(x)
formulas.whenToUse: Detecting burst errors on a frame using a generator polynomial.

terms.means: number of CRC/check bits = deg G(x)

formulas.trap: Append r zero bits BEFORE dividing; the remainder (not the quotient) is the CRC. A degree-r generator detects all bursts of length <= r.

formulas.name: Hamming Distance Detection/Correction
formulas.plain: Min Hamming distance d: detect up to d-1 errors, correct up to floor((d-1)/2)
formulas.whenToUse: Given the minimum Hamming distance of a code.

terms.means: minimum Hamming distance of the code

formulas.trap: Detection and correction can't be claimed simultaneously at max; correcting t needs d >= 2t+1.

formulas.name: Hamming Code Check Bits
formulas.plain: Need r parity bits to correct single-bit errors in m data bits: 2^r >= m + r + 1
formulas.trap: The +1 accounts for the no-error syndrome.

formulas.name: Parity Check
formulas.plain: Single parity bit = XOR of data bits; detects any odd number of bit errors
formulas.trap: Cannot detect an even number of errors, and cannot correct.

formulas.name: Bit Stuffing (HDLC)
formulas.plain: After five consecutive 1s the sender stuffs a 0; flag 01111110 delimits frames
formulas.whenToUse: Counting transmitted bits with flag-based framing.
formulas.trap: Stuffing happens on five 1s even if the sixth bit would not have formed a flag; receiver removes the 0 after five 1s.

formulas.name: CSMA/CD Minimum Frame Time
formulas.plain: Transmission time >= 2 x propagation time, so min frame length = 2 * Tprop * bandwidth
formulas.whenToUse: Finding minimum frame size so collisions are detected.
formulas.trap: The factor is 2 (round trip). Ethernet's 64-byte min frame comes from this.

formulas.name: CSMA/CD Efficiency
formulas.whenToUse: Throughput of a CSMA/CD (classic Ethernet) shared medium.
formulas.trap: Smaller a (longer frames / shorter cable) gives higher efficiency.
